name: The Magic If
steps: Stand at the threshold of the room or situation.
Ask: 'If I were {name}, how would I enter this room?'
Answer with your body first — posture, pace, eyes — then walk in that way.

name: Psychological Gesture
steps: Recall your persona's signature gesture.
Perform it fully and physically three times, exaggerated.
Shrink it to an invisible inner version you can fire anywhere.

name: Voice & Walk Drill
steps: Say one everyday sentence in the persona's voice — pace, volume, tone.
Walk ten steps as the persona walks.
Combine: walk and speak the sentence together.

name: Morning Rehearsal
steps: Close your eyes. See one specific scene from today as the persona.
Run the scene start to finish — what you say, how you stand, how it resolves.
Let the matching emotion rise and hold it in the body for 60 seconds.

name: Pattern Interrupt
steps: Mid-day, when you catch an old reaction starting, say: 'Old me or new me?'
Take one breath.
Choose one visible new-me action, however small.

name: Cognitive Reframe
steps: Catch the old thought and write it verbatim.
Ask: is it true? is it useful?
Write the persona's version and say it once out loud.

name: If-Then Intentions
steps: Pick one trigger situation you will definitely meet today.
Pre-load: 'IF [trigger], THEN I [persona response].'
Say it three times so it fires without deciding.

name: Physical Anchor
steps: During a rehearsal peak, press thumb and finger together (or touch your anchor object).
Repeat over several sessions until the touch recalls the state.
Fire the anchor right before hard moments.

name: Batman Effect
steps: When stuck or stressed, ask: 'What would {name} do?'
Answer in third person: '{name} would…'
Do that thing within two minutes.

name: Morning Premeditation
steps: Name the hardest moment likely today.
See yourself meeting it as the persona — calmly, prepared.
Decide your first sentence or move in advance.

name: Evening Review
steps: Score today in-character from 1–10.
Name one win as the persona and one slip into the old pattern.
Set one intention for tomorrow. No self-punishment — data only.

name: One Trait Per Week
steps: This week, only the focus trait gets deliberate practice.
Other traits ride along — no extra effort on them.
Next week, rotate. Depth beats breadth.

name: Deep Wear Session
steps: Sit. Two minutes: watch old-pattern thoughts pass without holding them.
Five minutes: build the persona from the feet up — walk, chest, voice, beliefs.
Three minutes: as the persona, mentally live tonight and tomorrow morning.

name: Emotion Laddering
steps: Name where you actually are right now, without judgment.
Find the feeling one rung up — not the summit, just one rung.
Do one small act that expresses that one-rung-up feeling.

name: Evidence Hunt
steps: Once today, catch one real moment that matched your persona or vision.
Log it in one sentence in your evidence log.
Notice the feeling of 'it's already starting'.
